abstract.text: All migration steps necessary in reading apps to upgrade to major versions of the Swift Readium toolkit will be documented in this file.

content.text: Removing the HTTP Server from the EPUB Navigator

inlineContent.text: The EPUB navigator no longer requires an HTTP server. Publication resources are now served directly to the web views using a custom URL scheme handler.
The PDF navigator still requires an HTTP server. If you are not using the PDF navigator, you can remove the

content.text: Migrating LCP Repositories from SQLite to the Keychain

inlineContent.text: provides built-in Keychain-based repositories that are more secure, persist across app reinstalls, and optionally synchronize across devices via iCloud Keychain.
Replace the SQLite repositories with their Keychain equivalents:
If your app already stores LCP data in the SQLite database, you can migrate it to the Keychain using the built-in migration helpers. Run this migration once, for example during an app update:

Migrating to the new Preferences API (see below) is required for the user settings to work with the new HTTP server.
is now deprecated and you don’t need to manage the HTTP server or register publications manually to it anymore.
Instead, the EPUB, PDF and CBZ navigators expect an instance of
upon creation. They will take care of registering and removing the publication automatically from the provided server.
You can implement your own HTTP server using a third-party library. But the easiest way to migrate is to use the one provided in the new Readium package

content.text: Upgrading to the new Preferences API

inlineContent.text: The 2.5.0 release introduces a brand new user preferences API for configuring the EPUB and PDF Navigators. This new API is easier and safer to use. To learn how to integrate it in your app,
If you integrated the EPUB navigator from a previous version, follow these steps to migrate:
Migrate the local HTTP server from your app,
Adapt your user settings interface to the new API using preferences editors. The
. The settings are not stored in the User Defaults by the toolkit anymore. Instead, you are responsible for persisting and restoring the user preferences as you see fit (e.g. as a JSON file).
If you want to migrate the legacy EPUB settings, you can use the helper
object after translating the existing user settings.
Please refer to the following table for the correspondence between legacy settings (from
helper to turn pages with the arrows and space keyboard keys or taps on the edge of the screen. To use it, you need to implement the following

code: func navigator(_ navigator: VisualNavigator, didTapAt point: CGPoint) {
func navigator(_ navigator: VisualNavigator, didPressKey event: KeyEvent) {

inlineContent.text: offers a lot of customization options, take a look at the type documentation.
With this new release, we migrated all the
The same Readium libraries are available as before, but you will need to update the configuration of your dependency manager.
With SPM, instead of having one Swift Package per Readium library, we now have a single Readium Swift Package offering one product per Readium library.
First, remove all the Readium Swift Packages from your project setting, from the tab
. Then, add the new Swift Package using the following URL
Xcode will then ask you which Package Product you want to add to your app. Add the ones you were using previously.
That’s all, your project should build successfully.
. Carthage will build all the Readium libraries and their dependencies, but you are free to add only the ones you are using as before. Take a look at the
If you are using CocoaPods, you will need to update the URL to the Podspecs in your
If you are integrating your own forks of the Readium modules, you will need to migrate them to a single fork and port your changes. Follow strictly the given steps and it should go painlessly.
Upgrade your forks to the latest Readium 2.2.0 version from the legacy repositories, as you would with any update. The 2.2.0 version is available on both the legacy repositories and the new
one. It will be used to port your changes over to the single repository.
In a new local directory, clone your legacy forks as well as the new single fork:
Reset the new fork to be in the same state as the 2.2.0 release.
For each Readium module, port your changes over to the new fork.

code: git commit -m "Apply local changes to Readium"

inlineContent.text: Finally, pull the changes to upgrade to the latest version of the fork. You might need to fix some conflicts.
Your fork is now ready! To integrate it in your app as a local Git clone or submodule, follow the instructions from the
Nothing to change in your app to upgrade from 2.0.0-beta.2 to the final 2.0.0 release! Please follow the relevant sections if you are upgrading from an older version.
. No change should be needed in your app though.
Note that all the APIs marked as deprecated in previous versions are now unavailable. You will need to follow the warning instructions if you were still using them.
This is the last beta before the final 2.0.0 release.

content.text: Using XCFrameworks to build with Xcode 12

inlineContent.text: This new version requires Xcode 12 and Carthage 0.37.0 to work and is now using XCFrameworks for its dependencies. You will need to upgrade your app to use XCFrameworks as well.
Migrating a project to XCFrameworks is

Add references to XCFrameworks in each of your targets:

works as a regular Carthage dependency, you do not need to use a submodule anymore.

content.text: Presenting a Protected Publication with a Navigator

inlineContent.text: In case the credentials were incorrect or missing, the
, but in a “restricted” state. This allows reading apps to import publications by accessing their metadata without having the passphrase.
But if you need to present the publication with a Navigator, you will need to first check if the

Besides missing credentials, a publication can be restricted if the Content Protection returned an error, for example when the publication is expired. In which case, you must display the error to the user by checking the presence of a

code: // A status error occurred, for example the publication expired
// User cancelled the unlocking, for example by dismissing a passphrase dialog.

content.text: Acquiring a Publication from an LCPL

inlineContent.text: , which returns a cancellable task. It doesn’t require the user to enter its passphrase anymore to download the publication.
You can integrate additional DRMs, such as Adobe ACS, by implementing the
protocol. This will provide first-class support for this DRM in the Streamer and Navigator.

abstract.text: The Readium Swift Toolkit is a toolkit for ebooks, audiobooks, and comics written in Swift & Kotlin.
The Readium Swift toolkit enables you to develop reading apps for iOS and iPadOS. It provides built-in support for multiple publication formats such as EPUB, PDF, audiobooks, and comics.

titleInlineContent.text: The Renew Loan LCP API got revamped

https://github.com/readium/r2-lcp-swift/.title: The Renew Loan LCP API got revamped
The dependency to R2LCPClient.framework was removed from r2-lcp-swift
